rulings.Case Title: CALDERA PHARMECEUTICALS, INC., A DELAWARE VS. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Rescheduling Of Hearing On Defts Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories And Requests For Admission From Caldera Pharamceuticals, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 24, 2013, Line 1: Cross Complainants THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S and LOS ALAMOS NATIONAL SECURITY, L.L.C.'S (Notice Of Rescheduling Of Hearing On Defendants') Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories And Requests For Admission From Caldera Pharamceuticals, Inc. Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear. The court does not have any reply briefs with respect to the motions to compel or the request for a discovery referee and assumes none were filed. Parties are to meet and confer with respect to what is at issue with respect to the motions to compel and how the court can be helpful it getting this case back on track so that discovery can be scheduled and completed prior to the currently scheduled trial date. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MIDLAND FUNDING LLC VS. BRUCE MAESTAS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, October 24, 2013, Line 4, PLAINTIFF MIDLAND FUNDING LLC's Hearing On Claim Of Exemption. Hearing required. The court is inclined to deny Mr. Maestas' claim of exemption on the ground that he has not shown that all of his earnings are exempt. (CCP § 703.580.) The Court is inclined to order $ 50.00 to be withheld from Debtor's earnings per pay period until the debt is satisfied. Mr. Maestas is requested to bring to the hearing a current pay stub showing his deductions, and to be prepared to support the amount of his expenses. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: VICTORIA NAVARRETE et al VS. ERIC CHUN KHUN LEE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Reopen Discovery For Sole Purpose To Compel Production Or Bar The Use Of Surveillance Evidence
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, October 24, 2013, Line 21, PLAINTIFFs VICTORIA NAVARRETE and FERNANDO NAVARRETE's Motion To Reopen Discovery For Sole Purpose To Compel Production Or Bar The Use Of Surveillance Evidence. Granted for the sole purpose of allowing Plaintiffs to make a motion compel production or bar the use of Defendants' surveillance. This evidence is relevant and directly responsive to form interrogatories. (CCP § 2024.050(b)(1).) Plaintiffs have sufficiently met and conferred prior to filing the instant motion. (CCP § 2024.050(b)(2).) Plaintiffs have demonstrated good cause for not bringing this motion earlier based on the disclosure of this evidence five days prior to the initial trial date. (Ibid.) The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: VICTORIA NAVARRETE et al VS. ERIC CHUN KHUN LEE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Production Or Bar The Use Of Surveillance Evidence; Compendium
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, October 24, 2013, Line 21, PLAINTIFFs VICTORIA NAVARRETE and FERNANDO NAVARRETE's Motion To Compel Production Or Bar The Use Of Surveillance Evidence; Compendium. Granted. Within 10 days, Defendant shall produce the surveillance evidence. This evidence is relevant and directly responsive to form interrogatories. (CCP § 2024.050(b)(1).) Plaintiffs have sufficiently met and conferred prior to filing the instant motion. (CCP § 2024.050(b)(2).) Plaintiffs have demonstrated good cause for not bringing this motion earlier based on the disclosure of this evidence five days prior to the initial trial date. (Ibid.) Defendant's argument that the surveillance evidence will only be used as impeachment evidence is not a bar to production of relevant evidence pursuant to properly served Form Interrogatories. Defendants' objection that the surveillance evidence is attorney work product is disingenuous. Defendant cannot claim work product to avoid disclosure of the relevant surveillance evidence and also intend to introduce it for impeachment purposes at trial. Such a claim would preclude its introduction of the evidence at trial. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: MT. TAM LASER AND SKIN CARE CORPORATION et al VS. RONALD P. GOLDMAN, A PROFESSIONAL LAW CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, October 24, 2013, Line 11, DEFENDANT RONALD P. GOLDMAN, A PROFESSIONAL LAW CORPORATION's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. Defendant's motion for judgment on the pleadings as to the first and fourth causes of action in Plaintiffs' Complaint is denied. Plaintiffs' and Defendant's requests for judicial notice are granted. Plaintiffs have alleged sufficient facts to state a claim for legal malpractice and breach of fiduciary duty. (See Complaint 44-64, 78-81; Coscia v. McKenna & Cuneo (2001) 25 Cal.4th 1194, 1199.) Taking the facts as true, but for Defendant's breaches of duty, Plaintiffs would not have been exposed to additional liability in the Killips matter and would not have incurred attorney's fees and costs defending the additional claims. (See Complaint 57-59.) It is a factual question whether Plaintiffs could have achieved a better result in the underlying action but for Defendant's alleged breach of duty. (See Lazy Acres Market v. Tseng 152 Cal.App.4th 1431, 1435 (2007).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CACH LLC VS. HELEN Y. LEE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, October 24, 2013, Line 12, DEFENDANT HELEN LEE's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. Denied. Defendant has not met the statutory requirements of CCP §473(b) or 473.5. As to 473(b), Defendant has not established that default was entered due to her "surprise." The term "surprise" as used in §473, refers to some condition or situation in which a party is unexpectedly placed to his injury, without any default or negligence of his own, which ordinary prudence could not have guarded against. (Hearn v. Howard (2009) 177 Cal.App.4th 1193, 1206.) Defendant has not established she acted with ordinary prudence. The affidavit of the process server states that he properly effectuated substituted service, by serving a competent member of the household and subsequently mailing the documents. Defendant has not established that she did not live at the residence at the time of service or that she did not regularly receive mail there. For the same reasons, Defendant has not established that her lack of actual notice was not caused by her avoidance of service or inexcusable neglect, as required by CCP §473.5. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JULIE WALKER VS. PACIFIC GAS & ELECTRIC COMPANY, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Completion Of Julie Walker'S Deposition; And For An Award Of Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 24, 2013, Line 5: Plaintiff JULIE WALKER'S Motion To Compel The Completion Of Julie Walker's Deposition; And For An Award Of Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear to discuss the scheduling for the completion of Plaintiff's deposition. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICIA RUIZ et al VS. EDWARD D. REISKIN, IN HIS OFFICIAL CAPACITY AS THE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, October 24, 2013, Line 17, DEFENDANTs EDWARD D. REISKIN, IN HIS OFFICIAL CAPACITY AS THE DIRECTOR OF TRANSPORTATION FOR THE SAN FRANCISCO MUNICIPAL TRANSPORTATION AGENCY and SAN FRANCISCO MUNICIPAL TRANSPORTATION AGENCY's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer is overruled. Plaintiff states a cause of action for breach of contract. The CBA agreement is enforceable under Freeman v. State Farm Mut. Auto Ins. Co. (1975) 14 Cal.3d 473, 480 and Retired Employees Assn. of Orange County Inc. v. County of Orange (2011) 52 Cal.4th 1171, 1182. The Court cannot determine that the claim is subject to the CBA's grievance process. Neither party asks the Court to take judicial notice of the CBA and Plantiff's interpretation of the CBA at 4:18-20 of the Complaint is entitled to deference. See Aragon-Haas v. Family Security Ins. Services, Inc., (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 232, 239 ("While plaintiffs' interpretation of the contract may prove invalid, it [is] improper to resolve the issue against her solely on her own pleading. 'In ruling on a demurrer, the likelihood that the pleading will be able to prove his allegations is not the question.'"). The demurrer to the PAGA cause of action is overruled. The Court cannot determine whether there is a conflict between the CBA and Labor Code because a complete copy of the CBA was not provided to the Court. The demurrer to the petition for writ of mandate cause of action is overruled. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
